stem: A software testing study surveys 25 test cases and observes 4 positive outcomes out of 25 (16.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (8 observations, rate = 12.5%) and Group B (17 observations, rate = 17.6%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 43% and 62% of the population. Estimate the true software bug rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.16. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.0994 (range [0.0863, 0.1139]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.064, 0.3465]. Point estimate: 0.0994.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.53: 0.53×0.031 + 0.47×0.1765 = 0.0994.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
